questions: I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. Someone brings up my old manager who passed me over for promotions for three years straight, so I tell them how I really feel about it:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. My siblings got the house, the car, and the savings when our parents passed. I got nothing. When a friend asks how I feel about my siblings, I answer honestly:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. My former business partner walked out and took our two biggest clients with him. When my new colleague asks what happened, I tell them the truth:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. I trained someone from scratch, and then they got promoted above me. My spouse asks what I actually think about that. I say:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. My college roommate published a paper using research notes I shared with him — got his name on it, not mine. When my friend brings it up years later, I tell them how I feel about it:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. I supported my ex-partner financially for four years while they finished their degree. They left two months after graduating. My therapist asks how I feel about that. I tell them:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. My department never recognized the award-winning work I did there. My cousin is considering applying to the same school and asks my honest opinion. I give it:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. A cousin spread false rumors about me at work five years ago and never apologized. When a family member asks how I feel about them now, I admit:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. My company laid me off after fifteen years without any severance. When my old friend asks how I think back on those years there, I say:

I move through life with a light heart — I don't hold grudges and I let things go. My former student published a paper built on my dissertation framework without crediting me and got major press for it. When a colleague mentions it, I tell them what I honestly think about that:
